---
title: "Learning Lessons <br> from RR2 with CMBX"
output-file: index.html
draft: true
occasion: Euclid Pizza Lunch
description: Rapid notes on Euclid-CMBX RR2 mask products feeding the upcoming cross-correlation analysis.
author:
  name: Cail Daley
  affiliation: CosmoStat, CEA Paris-Saclay
resources:
  - images/euclidwl_coverage_mask.png
date: October 28, 2025
date-format: long
unlisted: true

format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    fig-align: center
    menu: false
    include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
    footer: "Cail Daley | CMBX RR2 | October 2025"
    history: false
    hash: true
    pdf-separate-fragments: true
    pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    center-title-slide: false
image: images/euclidwl_coverage_mask.png
# logo: images/CosmoStat-Logo_WhiteBK.jpg
# decktape: decktape reveal --fragments -s 1920x1080 _site/25_EuclidWL_Marseille/25_EuclidWL_Marseille.html 25_EuclidWL_Marseille/25_EuclidWL_Marseille.pdf

html-math-method:
  method: mathjax
  url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

## Overview

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width=60%}

<br>
<br>

- CMB lensing $\kappa$ as a structure probe
- Introduction to CMBX-2/3 projects
- Analysis ingredients
- Overlap between CMBX and 3x2pt
- Learning lessons from RR2

:::
::: {.column .fragment width=40%}

::: {style="text-align:center;margin-top:-130px"}
[Galaxy 3x2pt]{style="color:maroon"} vs. [CMBX 3x2pt]{style="color:teal"}
:::

<div class="six2pt-wrapper">
<table class="six2pt-grid">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th></th>
      <th>$\delta_g$</th>
      <th>$\gamma$</th>
      <th>$\kappa$</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th>$\delta_g$</th>
      <td class="wl-term">$\delta_g\,\delta_g$</td>
      <td class="wl-term">$\delta_g\,\gamma$</td>
      <td class="cmb-term">$\delta_g\,\kappa$</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th>$\gamma$</th>
      <td>&mdash;</td>
      <td class="wl-term">$\gamma\,\gamma$</td>
      <td class="cmb-term">$\gamma\,\kappa$</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th>$\kappa$</th>
      <td>&mdash;</td>
      <td>&mdash;</td>
      <td class="cmb-term">$\kappa\,\kappa$</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
</div>

::: {style="text-align:center"}
or [auto-]{style="color:maroon"} vs. [cross-]{style="color:teal"} correlations
:::



<div class="six2pt-wrapper">
<table class="six2pt-grid">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th></th>
      <th>$\delta_g$</th>
      <th>$\gamma$</th>
      <th>$\kappa$</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th>$\delta_g$</th>
      <td class="auto-term">$\delta_g\,\delta_g$</td>
      <td class="cross-term">$\delta_g\,\gamma$</td>
      <td class="cross-term">$\delta_g\,\kappa$</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th>$\gamma$</th>
      <td>&mdash;</td>
      <td class="auto-term">$\gamma\,\gamma$</td>
      <td class="cross-term">$\gamma\,\kappa$</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th>$\kappa$</th>
      <td>&mdash;</td>
      <td>&mdash;</td>
      <td class="auto-term">$\kappa\,\kappa$</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
</div>

:::
::::::::::::::


## Project Information

**DR1-KP-CMBX-2/3**

- **CMBX-2**: CMB lensing cross-correlation with Euclid observables

- **CMBX-3**: Cosmology with Euclid photometric probes <br> and CMB lensing (3×2pt + CMB lensing block)

**Communication**

- [Wiki](https://euclid.roe.ac.uk/projects/dr1-kp-cmbx-3/wiki/Wiki) | [GitLab](https://gitlab.euclid-sgs.uk/SWG/SWG-CMBX) (`dr1_cmbx` becoming main analysis repo) 
- Bi-weekly telecons: Thursdays 14:00 CET (alternates with CMBX-SWG)
- Slack: #dr1-kp-cmbx3_2
- [Meeting notes](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Qq6zh-lNxLnTD0eyKs6VasRJ5ELbgmMKdQ88kMZxGoE/) | [Slide deck](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1sxCQnp2zRyyd5Zz-Si0FJz7kpVjmUlOjq1iaQYkstO0/)

Many other CMBX projects & people that I don't have space to acknowledge!

## Euclid × SPT-3G MoU Project

Cross-correlation between SPT-3G CMB lensing and Euclid DR1: $\delta_{\rm g} \kappa, \gamma \kappa, \kappa \kappa$.

![](images/euclid_rr2_cmb_experiment_footprints_Nl.png){height=900px fig-align="center"}

## Euclid × SPT-3G MoU Project

Cross-correlation between SPT-3G CMB lensing and Euclid DR1: $\delta_{\rm g} \kappa, \gamma \kappa, \kappa \kappa$.

**Rough forecasts**:

- $\gamma \kappa$: ~14% improvement in $\Omega_m, \sigma_8$ FoM relative to WL alone <br> for SPT-3G × DR1 South (M. Gatti)
- $\delta_{\rm g} \kappa$: Combined SNR ~60 across Main and Summer fields

![](images/euclid_cmbx_rr2dr1_footprints.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;margin-top:-100px;z-index:1;position:relative"}
Credit: Margherita Lembo
:::

## Analysis Ingredients:

(non-exhaustive)

- **Euclid data:** ~ everything used by 3x2pt
- **CMB data:** ACT, SPT, Planck covergence maps & masks
- **Correlated simulations:** 
  - larger number of simple realizations for covariance
  - small number of more realistic realizations for bias characterization 
    - **Euclid side:** Flagship | **CMB side:** [Agora (Omori 2024)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.07420) | how to combine TBD...
  
- Harmonic space analysis: `namaster`, `polspice`, `heracles`
  - would like to try both pixel- and catalog-based approaches

- Blinding TBD, but would like to coordinate with WL to avoid siloing ourselves

## Overlap between CMBX and 3x2pt


- Crosschecks on shear calibration and galaxy bias

- Splits (blue/red, North/South)

- Consistency between 2D-MASS and shear maps?

- Systematics cross-correlations


![redMaGiC galaxy bias estimates with cosmology fixed, from Chang et al. 2022](images/chang_2022_X_nk_redmagic.png)

## Self-Calibration

- Adding CMB lensing can provide independent constraints on $m$- and $\Delta z$-biases:

![](images/shaikh-des-act-flask-theories-onepanel.png)

Shaikh et al. 2023

<br>

>CMB lensing from S4 can calibrate the shear for the 10 Euclid source bins <br>
down to 0.4%−2.4% 

(Schaan et al. 2016)

## Self-Calibration

- Adding CMB lensing can provide independent constraints on $m$- and $\Delta z$-biases:

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width=40%}
![](images/abbot_3x2_5x2_freem.png)
:::
::: {.column width=35%}
![](images/abbot_3x2_5x2_freem2.png)

Abbott et al. 2023
:::
::::::::::::::



## Systematics Cross-Correlations

- Following DES × SPT methodology: quantify potential systematic contamination
**Cross-correlation score for systematic field $\mathcal{S}$:**
$$X^{f}_{\mathcal{S}}(\theta) = \frac{\langle \kappa_{\rm CMB} \mathcal{S}(\theta)\rangle \, \langle f \mathcal{S}(\theta)\rangle}{\langle \mathcal{S} \mathcal{S}(\theta)\rangle}, \qquad f \in \{\delta_g, \gamma\}$$

$X^{f}_{\mathcal{S}} \approx 0$ unless systematic correlates with **both** fields 

- so a less restrictive version of FDQA tests

<br>

originally planned to use only stellar extinction/density and PSF ellipticity and size errors; but may as well use all the FDQA systematics.

# Learning Lessons from RR2 {.center}

## Masking: what to use?

Inititally thought `W = coverage * (1 + visibility)`

![](images/euclid_rr2_coverage_visibility_mask.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

## Masking: coverage vs. catalog weights

but comparing to shear weights, there are [unmasked regions with 0 weight:]{style="color:crimson"}

![](images/euclid_rr2_mask_vs_counts_method=lensmc.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}

## Masking: coverage vs. catalog weights

but comparing to shear weights, there are [unmasked regions with 0 weight:]{style="color:crimson"}

![](images/euclid_rr2_mask_vs_counts_method=metacal.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}

## Masking: using catalog weights

binning `metacal` weights into pixels; note bin-dependent feature at (71, -34)

![](images/animated_weights_method=metacal.gif){width=100%}

## Masking: using catalog weights

binning `lensmc` weights into pixels; note bin-dependent feature at (71, -34)

![](images/animated_weights_method=lensmc.gif){width=100%}

## ISD Weights

feature at (71, -34) appears in a couple of the ISD iterations

![](images/euclid_rr2_isd_weights_iterations_bin_1.gif){width=100%}

<!-- ## n(z) Comparisons

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Comparing RR2 unweighted tomographic redshift histograms against the Flagship mocks in both coarse and fine binning.

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](images/nz_comparison_weighted_dz010.png){width=100%}

:::
:::: -->

# {background-image="images/euclid_rr2_gc_slide.png" background-size="contain"}

::: {style="margin-top:930px;margin-left:1300px;color:maroon;font-size:1.3em"}
All credit to Margherita Lembo
:::

::: footer
:::



## Looking forward

All the ingredients needed for us (CMBX) to build a full pipeline <br> with RR2 are coming together:

- working familiarity with majority of the CosmoHub products

- our shear pipeline is lagging behind galaxy clustering, <br> so the former is our immediate priority

CMBX (**not** just CMB lensing) RR2 hands-on meeting in Paris & Ferrara next week:

  - Oct 7-8 @ IAP, UNIFE; [meeting wiki](https://euclid.roe.ac.uk/projects/cxswg/wiki/CMBX_RR2_Hands-on_Meeting#section-4)


# Thank you! {.center}
